Tool_tcs_Description: Tax Collected at Source on LRS remittance, sale of goods, and scrap — illustrative section-wise rates.
Tool_tcs_Subtitle: Section-wise TCS on remittance, goods & scrap
Tool_tcs_LearnText: TCS is collected by the seller or authorised dealer and remitted to the government. Common cases include foreign remittance under LRS (5% or 20% by amount), sale of goods above ₹50 lakh at 0.1%, and scrap at 1%.
Tool_tcs_FormulaNote: Illustrative planning figures. LRS education/medical exceptions, PAN rules, and FY rate changes are simplified — confirm with your bank or tax professional.
Tool_tcs_NatureHint_lrs_foreign: Simplified: 5% if remittance ≤ ₹7 lakh; 20% if above.
Tool_tcs_AmountHint: Remittance, sale consideration, or scrap sale value.
Tool_tcs_Result_ThresholdFootnote: Where TCS / higher rate kicks in
Tool_tcs_Status_Below: Amount is at or below the threshold of {0} — no TCS in this simplified view.
Tool_tcs_ExampleStep_2: ₹10 lakh is above the ₹7 lakh LRS threshold in this model.
Tool_tcs_ExampleStep_4: If amount were ₹5 lakh (≤ ₹7L), TCS would be 5% = ₹25,000.
Tool_tcs_ExampleStep_5: Sale of goods uses 0.1% only above ₹50 lakh; scrap uses 1% with no threshold here.
Tool_tcs_Step_1: Choose nature: LRS, sale of goods, or scrap.
Tool_tcs_Step_2: Enter the remittance or sale amount.
Tool_tcs_Step_3: Apply the nature’s rate and threshold (LRS uses 5%/20%).
Tool_tcs_Step_4: TCS = amount × rate when applicable; net = amount − TCS.
Tool_tcs_Info_Purpose: Estimate TCS on common India transactions by nature and amount.
Tool_tcs_Info_WhyKnow: Banks and sellers collect TCS upfront — knowing the outflow helps cash planning.
Tool_tcs_Info_RealLife: Sending money abroad under LRS, large B2B goods sales, or scrap dealer invoices.
Tool_tcs_Info_Goal_2: See TCS and net after collection
Tool_tcs_Info_Goal_3: Compare lower vs higher amount scenarios
Tool_tcs_Info_Alt_Tds_When: Tax deducted by payer (salary, interest, rent).
Tool_tcs_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows LRS 5%/20%, goods 0.1%, and scrap 1% in one TCS view.
Tool_tcs_Info_Motto: Know what gets collected before you remit or sell.
Tool_tds_on_property_Description: Section 194IA: 1% TDS when property consideration exceeds ₹50 lakh — with joint buyer share note.
Tool_tds_on_property_LearnText: When you buy immovable property (other than agricultural land) for more than ₹50 lakh, Section 194IA requires the buyer to deduct 1% TDS on the consideration and deposit it. Joint buyers still deduct on the full amount — this tool shows an equal-share note for planning.
Tool_tds_on_property_FormulaNote: Illustrative 194IA view. Stamp duty value vs agreement value, PAN of seller, and Form 26QB timelines are simplified — confirm with your CA or the Income Tax portal.
Tool_tds_on_property_ConsiderationHint: TDS applies when this exceeds {0} (₹50 lakh).
Tool_tds_on_property_RateHint: Default 1% with PAN; higher if PAN missing (override here).
Tool_tds_on_property_BuyersHint: Joint buyers: TDS is still on full consideration; share is illustrative only.
Tool_tds_on_property_Result_ShareFootnot: Equal split of consideration across {0} buyer(s)
Tool_tds_on_property_Status_Below: Consideration is at or below {0} — no 194IA TDS in this view.
Tool_tds_on_property_Status_Applied: 194IA applies at {0} → TDS ≈ {1}. Deposit via Form 26QB.
Tool_tds_on_property_JointNote: Joint buyers: each may deposit their share of TDS, but the rate applies to the full consideration — not to each share separately for the threshold test.
Tool_tds_on_property_ScenarioSubtitle: At threshold vs your inputs vs joint buyers
Tool_tds_on_property_ExampleStep_4: With 2 joint buyers, TDS is still ₹60,000 on the full amount; equal share of consideration ≈ ₹30 lakh each.
Tool_tds_on_property_ExampleStep_5: Deposit TDS and file Form 26QB within the prescribed timeline.
Tool_tds_on_property_Step_1: Enter consideration (and optional rate / buyer count).
Tool_tds_on_property_Step_4: Joint buyers: note equal share; TDS still on full value.
Tool_tds_on_property_Info_Purpose: Estimate 1% TDS when buying property above ₹50 lakh.
Tool_tds_on_property_Info_WhyKnow: Buyers must deduct and deposit TDS — missing it delays registration and attracts interest.
Tool_tds_on_property_Info_RealLife: Buying a flat or plot above ₹50 lakh, including joint purchases with family.
Tool_tds_on_property_Info_Goal_2: Compute TDS and net to seller
Tool_tds_on_property_Info_Alt_Tds_When: Other TDS sections in one place.
Tool_tds_on_property_Info_Differentiatio: FinYatra focuses on 194IA with threshold, rate override, and joint-buyer share.
Tool_tds_on_property_Info_Motto: Deduct 1% before you register the sale.
Tool_tds_on_rent_Description: Section 194IB: 2% TDS when monthly rent exceeds ₹50,000 — annualise and Form 26QC note.
Tool_tds_on_rent_LearnText: Individuals and HUFs (not subject to tax audit) paying rent above ₹50,000 a month must deduct 2% TDS under Section 194IB and deposit it using Form 26QC. This tool annualises monthly TDS for cash planning.
Tool_tds_on_rent_FormulaNote: Illustrative 194IB view. Company/firm tenants may use 194I instead. Confirm due dates and Form 26QC on the Income Tax portal.
Tool_tds_on_rent_RentHint: TDS applies when monthly rent exceeds {0}.
Tool_tds_on_rent_RateHint: Default 2% (194IB). Override if your case differs.
Tool_tds_on_rent_Status_Below: Monthly rent is at or below {0} — no 194IB TDS in this view.
Tool_tds_on_rent_Form26qcNote: Deposit TDS and file Form 26QC (challan-cum-statement) for each month as required.
Tool_tds_on_rent_ScenarioSubtitle: At threshold vs your rent vs +25%
Tool_tds_on_rent_ExampleStep_5: File Form 26QC and pay TDS for each applicable month.
Tool_tds_on_rent_Step_4: Remember Form 26QC for deposit and reporting.
Tool_tds_on_rent_Info_Purpose: Estimate 2% TDS when monthly rent exceeds ₹50,000.
Tool_tds_on_rent_Info_WhyKnow: Tenants must deduct TDS monthly — missing Form 26QC attracts interest and fees.
Tool_tds_on_rent_Info_RealLife: Paying high house or office rent as an individual or HUF tenant.
Tool_tds_on_rent_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra annualises 194IB TDS and flags Form 26QC in one rent view.
Tool_tds_on_rent_Info_Motto: If rent crosses ₹50k, plan the 2% TDS.
